title: rubus ulmifolius var. calcareus
intro: Rubus ulmifolius var. calcareus is a rare and lesser-known variant of the bramble plant, characterized by its unique leaf shape and habitat adaptation to calcareous soils. Traditionally, this plant is used in herbal medicine, with its medicinal properties attributed to its high content of anthocyanins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rubus ulmifolius var. calcareus are believed to include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which may help protect against cell damage, reduce inflammation, and prevent infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be consulted with a medical professional before consumption.
